title: M0 Foundation and standards
description: Bootstrap repository architecture, toolchain conventions, quality gate commands, and CI baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Contributor can bootstrap and run lint/typecheck/test via documented path; CI runs same gates
close_reason: M0 foundation completed: repository skeleton exists, canonical make-based quality gates are defined and documented, CI runs the same gate path via make check, and contributor bootstrap runbooks are in place.

title: M1 Traceability core: spec and trace validation
description: Implement deterministic spec ID parsing, trace marker parsing, coverage, and validation outputs.
acceptance_criteria: dp trace coverage and dp trace validate exist with stable JSON + exit codes; parser edge-case tests pass
close_reason: M1 traceability core completed: dp trace coverage and dp trace validate implemented with stable JSON and deterministic exit behavior, plus parser/validator edge-case tests passing in make check.

title: DP-Codex v1: Codex-native disciplined process toolkit
description:
Deliver a Codex-first disciplined workflow toolkit with deterministic CLI, enforcement, and migration path.

Scope source: docs/EXECUTION-PLAN.md
Definition of done: all milestone exit criteria and v1 release readiness checks pass.
acceptance_criteria: All milestones M0-M6 satisfy exit criteria in docs/EXECUTION-PLAN.md; full loop demo completes in clean repo; pilot migration validated
close_reason: Delivered DP-Codex v1 scope end-to-end across M0-M6 with empirical pilot validation, enforcement/CI parity, migration runbooks, property-based testing, and release readiness evidence.

title: SPEC-80.19 end-to-end human and agent campaign pilot
description: Build a realistic end-to-end pilot and adoption experience that proves dp can take a non-dp primary spec through campaign init/refine/run/evidence/verify/recover for both a human operator and a Codex-like agent.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.19 defines the pilot scenario, user journeys, success metrics, and friction metrics. 2. A realistic fixture primary spec outside dp-internals exercises child specs, ADRs, validators, tests, Beads issues, goal contracts, evidence plans, loop next, supervised run, blocker routing, evidence verification, and recovery. 3. A script or test suite runs the full flow in an isolated repo and emits stable summary JSON/markdown. 4. README and runbooks provide one canonical primary-spec-to-first-verified-goal path for human users and agents. 5. make check and the pilot pass.

title: SPEC-80.18 Beads lifecycle synchronization for campaigns
description: Integrate campaign/goal lifecycle transitions with Beads so Beads remains the issue/dependency substrate instead of a one-time materialization target.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.18 defines how campaign nodes map to Beads epics/issues, dependency edges, claim/start/block/release/verify transitions, and follow-up creation. 2. dp can create or update Beads dependencies from LoopLedger edges without duplicating existing graph state. 3. Goal lifecycle transitions can update or propose Beads status/notes in deterministic, explicit write modes. 4. Blocker routing creates linked Beads follow-ups and records IDs in goal/campaign events. 5. Tests mock bd behavior for create/update/dep edges/failures, docs cover current Beads 1.0 behavior, and make check passes.

title: SPEC-80.17 evidence run artifacts and goal verification orchestration
description: Make evidence production and goal verification ergonomic enough for humans and agents: dp should produce durable evidence-run artifacts at predictable paths and provide a single goal verification orchestration command without weakening deterministic evidence gates.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.17 defines evidence-run artifact path conventions, provenance, retention, and goal-verification orchestration. 2. dp evidence run supports an explicit output path or artifact write mode that records stable JSON without shell redirection dependence. 3. dp verify --goal <goal.json> --json orchestrates goal lint, evidence lint, evidence run or evidence artifact validation, trace/provenance checks, and goal verification without model judgment. 4. Emitted Codex handoffs include concrete evidence artifact paths and exact complete/verify commands. 5. Tests cover successful verification, stale/mismatched evidence, failed checks, output path safety, docs, schemas, and make check.

title: SPEC-80.16 semantic campaign graph readiness
description: Harden primary-spec campaign compilation from draft scaffold into a ready dependency graph. Convert deterministic and LLM-authored refinement metadata into validated dependency edges, ready goal nodes, child specs, ADRs, evidence plans, and campaign readiness state without using model judgment in gates.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.16 defines ready-state gates for CampaignManifest, LoopLedger, GoalContracts, EvidencePlans, child specs, ADRs, and Beads links. 2. dp campaign refine or a dedicated readiness command can promote a draft campaign to ready only when deterministic lints and graph checks pass. 3. Dependency edges are explicit, acyclic, and traceable to deterministic cues, imported LLM metadata, or human-authored artifact references. 4. The compiler supports realistic rich primary specs better than heading-per-goal placeholders and records unresolved questions as blockers. 5. Tests cover graph promotion, invalid dependencies, missing validators, LLM metadata back-pressure, docs, schemas, and make check.

title: SPEC-80.15 campaign events and resume handoff
description: Add first-class campaign/loop progress events and resume-oriented handoff so future agent sessions can recover active or stale campaign work without chat memory. This fills the gap between goal events and campaign-level operational state.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.15 documents campaign event semantics, current_goal behavior, stale claim recovery, and resume handoff invariants. 2. dp campaign recover/status/run can surface active claimed work, stale leases, blocked nodes, and next recommended action in stable JSON. 3. Campaign or loop event records capture claim/start/block/release/verify rollups without hidden state. 4. A Codex session can recover an active claimed goal for the same agent or choose the next safe action without relying on chat history. 5. Tests cover clean resume, stale lease, blocked campaign, no-ready work, docs, schemas, and make check.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.15 campaign events and resume handoff. Added SPEC/ADR, append-only .dp/campaigns/events.jsonl handoff events, deterministic campaign status/recover resume packages, stale-claim reporting, blocker/evidence-pending/ready/verified resume actions, and supervised campaign run behavior that resumes active claims instead of claiming over them. Updated README, reference docs, runbook, schema, and SPEC-80 status. Verified with pytest tests/test_campaign_recovery.py tests/test_campaign_manifest.py tests/test_campaign_run.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, make test/lint/typecheck/check with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache, dp doctor --json, bd --readonly status --json, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, and dp verify --json.

title: SPEC-80.14 deterministic blocker artifact routing
description: Implement the SPEC-80 blocker-routing law: when a goal blocks with a known reason and --write-artifact, dp creates the next disciplined artifact from the GoalContract blocked_routes entry and optionally creates a Beads follow-up. This closes the gap between structured blocked state and durable recovery artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.14 documents blocker routing semantics and invariants before coding. 2. dp goal block <goal.json> --reason <known> --write-artifact --json validates the GoalContract, records a blocked event, writes the route-specific artifact stub for needs_specification, needs_decision, or needs_validator, and records artifact metadata in the event. 3. When the route asks for also_create_beads_issue, dp creates or records a Beads follow-up using current bd create semantics, with deterministic failure handling that does not hide the blocked event. 4. Artifact paths are sane, collision-safe, and stable enough for recovery; repeated blocks should not overwrite changed artifacts. 5. Tests cover artifact creation, Beads mocked creation, unsupported/missing routes, collision handling, docs, and make check.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.14 deterministic blocker artifact routing. Added SPEC/ADR, dp goal block --write-artifact, deterministic spec/ADR/EvidencePlan route artifacts, optional Beads follow-up creation with explicit failure metadata, routed blocker event reconstruction, Codex/loop handoff block commands, docs/README updates, and tests for artifact creation, mocked Beads success/failure, missing routes, and collision safety. Verified with pytest tests/test_goal_block_routing.py tests/test_goal_state.py tests/test_goal_emit.py tests/test_loop_ledger.py, make test/lint/typecheck/check with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache, dp doctor --json, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, and dp verify --json.

title: SPEC-70.07 Make Beads sync capability detection version-aware
description: Make dp's Beads health path version-aware so current Beads 1.0+ installations are not probed for removed bd sync behavior and do not emit stale sync warnings as health noise. Preserve backward compatibility for older/unknown Beads versions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-70.07 documents version-aware Beads capability behavior. 2. dp doctor --json does not invoke bd sync --help for parsed Beads versions where sync is known removed. 3. Current Beads 1.0+ reports sync_command_available=false without treating that as a warning. 4. Older or unparseable Beads versions may still probe bd sync --help for compatibility. 5. Unit tests cover current, legacy, and unavailable bd behavior. 6. make check passes.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-70.07 Beads version-aware capability detection. dp doctor now parses bd version, does not run bd sync --help for Beads 1.0+ where sync is known removed, reports sync_command_available=false without stale warnings for current versions, and preserves sync probing for older or unparseable versions. Verified with pytest tests/unit/test_beads_provider.py tests/unit/test_cli_task.py, dp doctor --json, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, and dp verify --json.

title: Clarify README goal handoff examples are reusable
description: Adjust README wording so goal-contract and Codex handoff examples describe using dp in any adopting repo/project, while keeping dp-codex fixture commands clearly framed as contributor smoke tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1. README Quick Start no longer centers the dp-codex SPEC-70.01 fixture as the main user story. 2. README explains goal contracts are repo/project artifacts. 3. Any checked-in fixture commands are clearly labeled contributor smoke tests. 4. make check and docs health checks pass.
close_reason: Reframed README goal handoff examples around adopting repositories and moved the dp-codex SPEC-70.01 fixture into contributor smoke-test wording. Verified with make check, dp trace validate --json, and dp verify --json.

title: Audit README and docs for SPEC-80 goal surfaces
description: Audit and update README and documentation indexes/references so they accurately describe the current dp-codex command surface after the SPEC-80 goal contract, lifecycle, and emission foundation landed.
acceptance_criteria: 1. README reflects current workflows, including goal contracts/lifecycle/emission and accurate project status. 2. Docs indexes and reference pages link the new goal docs and schema surfaces. 3. Stale or misleading claims about implemented campaign/evidence/runner features are corrected or explicitly marked as future work. 4. Verification commands are run and pass: relevant docs checks, dp review --json, dp verify --json, and make check.
close_reason: Updated README and documentation indexes/references for current SPEC-80 goal surfaces; added goal workflow runbook; clarified future campaign/evidence/runner work as not yet implemented; added goal-lint schema validation coverage. Verified with pytest tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, and dp verify --json.

title: SPEC-80.05 Evidence plan linter
description: Implement dp evidence lint for typed evidence plans after goal contracts and goal lifecycle commands are stable. Do not execute evidence in this slice.
acceptance_criteria: 1. EvidencePlan schema is documented. 2. dp evidence lint <evidence.json> --json validates argv arrays, registered or allowlisted checks, explicit timeouts, success exit codes, typed assertions, and mutation policy. 3. Raw shell strings are rejected. 4. Golden fixtures and tests cover valid and invalid plans. 5. make check passes.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic EvidencePlan lint via dp evidence lint with documented schema, stable JSON and exit semantics, registered argv validation, timeout/success-code/assertion/mutation-policy checks, raw-shell rejection in argv entries, golden fixtures, output schema tests, README/runbook/reference/spec updates. Verified with dp evidence lint tests/fixtures/evidence/valid_spec_80_05.json --json, pytest tests/test_evidence_lint.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, dp verify --json, and dp doctor --json.

title: SPEC-80.01 GoalContract schema and deterministic lint
description: Implement the first SPEC-80 slice: typed GoalContract loading and deterministic dp goal lint with stable JSON, explicit exit semantics, fixtures, tests, and reference docs.
acceptance_criteria: 1. docs/specs/SPEC-80-01-goal-contracts.md defines the contract. 2. dp goal lint <goal.json> --json validates supported schema, required fields, evidence, terminal states, boundaries, raw-shell prohibitions, trace IDs, blocker routes, and self-report success. 3. Golden valid and invalid fixtures cover the requested cases. 4. pytest tests/test_goal_lint.py and make check pass.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic GoalContract lint with stable JSON and exit codes, golden fixtures, reference docs, SPEC-80.01, and ADR boundaries. Verified with dp goal lint tests/fixtures/goals/valid_spec_70_01.json --json, pytest tests/test_goal_lint.py tests/test_goal_state.py tests/test_goal_emit.py, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, and dp trace coverage --json.

title: SPEC-80.03 Codex-operable goal emission
description: Implement dp goal emit and dp agent prompt for Codex-operable goal handoffs once goal contracts and state commands are in place.
acceptance_criteria: 1. docs/specs/SPEC-80-03-goal-emission.md defines prompt contents. 2. dp goal emit <goal.json> --format codex --json returns objective, evidence, constraints, boundaries, iteration policy, blocked-stop condition, and lifecycle commands. 3. dp agent prompt --goal <goal.json> --format codex --json delegates to the same contract. 4. pytest tests/test_goal_emit.py and make check pass.
close_reason: Implemented Codex-operable goal emission via dp goal emit and dp agent prompt over valid GoalContracts, including lifecycle commands and evidence/no-self-report instructions. Verified with dp goal emit tests/fixtures/goals/valid_spec_70_01.json --format codex --json, pytest tests/test_goal_emit.py, and make check.

title: SPEC-80.02 Append-only goal lifecycle state
description: Implement goal status, claim, start, heartbeat, block, and release over append-only JSONL events after the goal linter is real.
acceptance_criteria: 1. docs/specs/SPEC-80-02-goal-state-machine.md defines state transitions. 2. dp goal status/claim/start/heartbeat/block/release emit stable JSON and append events under .dp/goals/events.jsonl. 3. Claims have leases and stale leases can be detected. 4. State reconstructs from events. 5. pytest tests/test_goal_state.py and make check pass.
close_reason: Implemented append-only goal lifecycle state commands for status, claim, start, heartbeat, block, release, and evidence-pending complete over .dp/goals/events.jsonl. Verified with pytest tests/test_goal_state.py and make check.

title: SPEC-80 Agent campaign control plane
description: Implement SPEC-80 in small deterministic slices so dp can turn primary specs into durable agent-operable campaigns without adding LLM judgment to blocking gates.
acceptance_criteria: Goal contracts, append-only goal state, Codex prompt emission, loop ledger, evidence-plan lint, and campaign surfaces land as separately verified increments with docs, tests, stable JSON, and explicit exit semantics.
notes:
2026-06-27: Landed first agent-operable foundation: SPEC-80.01 GoalContract lint, SPEC-80.02 append-only goal lifecycle state, and SPEC-80.03 Codex goal emission. 2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.05 EvidencePlan lint with deterministic dp evidence lint, stable JSON/exit semantics, argv-only registered command validation, typed assertions, golden fixtures, docs/schema updates, and make check verification. 2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.04 LoopLedger lint/status/next-goal scheduling with acyclic dependencies, blocked/claimed skipping, verified dependency unlocks, claim leases through goal events, Codex package emission, docs/schema updates, and make check verification. 2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.06 CampaignManifest lint/status/recover. dp campaign lint validates manifest closure over primary spec, goals, evidence plans, loops, specs, ADRs, and Beads references; status derives campaign state from the current loop and append-only goal events; recover reports whether state can be reconstructed from repo artifacts without chat memory. 2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.07 conservative primary-spec campaign scaffold. dp campaign init --primary-spec <path> --write --json hashes a local primary spec, extracts Markdown headings, writes draft CampaignManifest/LoopLedger/GoalContract/EvidencePlan artifacts plus needs_refinement markers, refuses to overwrite changed artifacts, and lints generated artifacts without LLM calls or evidence execution. Verified with targeted tests, schema tests, temp-dir smoke, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate/coverage, and dp verify. Remaining work is tracked in open children for evidence executor safety and later LLM/supervised-runner work.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.08 controlled evidence executor. dp evidence run now gates execution behind dp evidence lint, runs registered argv checks with shell=False, declared timeouts, existing relative cwd values, controlled environment allowlist, typed assertions, stable JSON output, and explicit exit semantics. Unsafe raw-shell and unregistered evidence plans are denied before execution. Verified with dp evidence run fixture, pytest tests/test_evidence_run.py, make test/lint/typecheck/check, dp trace validate/coverage, dp verify, and dp doctor. Filed dpcx-pb5.10 for evidence-run-to-verified-goal integration; LLM/refinement/supervised runner work remains dpcx-pb5.9.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.10 evidence verification into goal state. dp goal verify consumes a matching successful dp evidence run artifact, checks goal id, evidence id, EvidencePlan path and sha256 freshness, rejects self-report JSON, and appends verified only on deterministic success. Loop dependencies continue to unlock only from reconstructed verified state. Verified with targeted pytest, make test/lint/typecheck/check, dp trace validate/coverage, dp verify, dp doctor, and bd --readonly status. Remaining SPEC-80 work is dpcx-pb5.9: semantic primary-spec campaign compilation, authoring-time LLM refinement with deterministic back-pressure, and any supervised runner as a thin adapter over the manual protocol.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.09 deterministic primary-spec semantic signals via dpcx-pb5.9.1. dp campaign init now emits compiler.mode=deterministic_markdown_signals with requirement/evidence/decision/blocker/dependency cues, node classifications, refinement states, and summary counts; generated campaign/loop/goal/refinement artifacts carry compiler provenance while remaining draft. No LLM calls, Beads writes, evidence execution, or prose-derived dependency edges were introduced. Remaining SPEC-80 work is authoring-time semantic refinement, optional LLM refinement with deterministic back-pressure, and any supervised runner.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.11 deterministic campaign refine through dpcx-pb5.9.2. Campaign init signals can now be materialized into child spec/ADR stubs, GoalContract/EvidencePlan refinement metadata, and explicit Beads work without LLM calls or evidence execution. Remaining SPEC-80 work is actual LLM-assisted refinement with provider provenance, semantic graph hardening, and any supervised runner after the manual protocol remains stable.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.12 agent-mediated LLM refinement through dpcx-pb5.9.3. dp can now hand a campaign-refinement request to the calling agent provider and import a response artifact through deterministic validation/provenance while keeping gates LLM-free and campaign state draft. Remaining SPEC-80 work is semantic graph hardening and any supervised runner after the manual protocol remains stable.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.13 supervised campaign run step. dp campaign run --driver codex --supervised is now a one-step adapter over CampaignManifest status and LoopLedger next-goal protocol: it claims one ready goal, emits the Codex package, returns lifecycle commands, and stops without launching Codex, running evidence, or marking work verified.
2026-06-27: Planned remaining SPEC-80 end-to-end work as explicit Beads children so it does not remain implicit in chat: dpcx-pb5.11/SPEC-80.14 blocker artifact routing; dpcx-pb5.12/SPEC-80.15 campaign events and resume handoff; dpcx-pb5.13/SPEC-80.16 semantic campaign graph readiness; dpcx-pb5.14/SPEC-80.17 evidence run artifacts and goal verification orchestration; dpcx-pb5.15/SPEC-80.18 Beads lifecycle synchronization; dpcx-pb5.16/SPEC-80.19 end-to-end human and agent campaign pilot; dpcx-pb5.17/SPEC-80.20 managed supervised loop and launch adapters; dpcx-pb5.18/SPEC-80.21 primary spec intake UX and source adapters.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.14 deterministic blocker artifact routing through dpcx-pb5.11. dp goal block --write-artifact now resolves supported GoalContract blocked_routes into spec, ADR, or EvidencePlan artifacts, optionally creates Beads follow-ups, records routing metadata in append-only blocked events, and emits artifact-producing block commands in Codex/loop handoffs. Remaining open SPEC-80 work is dpcx-pb5.12/SPEC-80.15 campaign events and resume handoff; dpcx-pb5.13/SPEC-80.16 semantic campaign graph readiness; dpcx-pb5.14/SPEC-80.17 evidence run artifacts and goal verification orchestration; dpcx-pb5.15/SPEC-80.18 Beads lifecycle synchronization; dpcx-pb5.16/SPEC-80.19 end-to-end human and agent campaign pilot; dpcx-pb5.17/SPEC-80.20 managed supervised loop and launch adapters; dpcx-pb5.18/SPEC-80.21 primary spec intake UX and source adapters.
2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-80.15 campaign events and resume handoff through dpcx-pb5.12. dp campaign status/recover now emit deterministic resume packages for active claims, stale claims, evidence-pending goals, blockers, ready work, verified campaigns, and no-ready states. dp campaign run --supervised now resumes active claims instead of claiming over them and records new supervised claims in .dp/campaigns/events.jsonl. Remaining open SPEC-80 work is dpcx-pb5.13/SPEC-80.16 semantic campaign graph readiness; dpcx-pb5.14/SPEC-80.17 evidence run artifacts and goal verification orchestration; dpcx-pb5.15/SPEC-80.18 Beads lifecycle synchronization; dpcx-pb5.16/SPEC-80.19 end-to-end human and agent campaign pilot; dpcx-pb5.17/SPEC-80.20 managed supervised loop and launch adapters; dpcx-pb5.18/SPEC-80.21 primary spec intake UX and source adapters.

title: M7.0 Fix dp-codex runtime install dependency
description: The machine-level uv tool install exposes dp globally, but dp imports jsonschema at runtime while pyproject declares jsonschema only in the dev group. Move the dependency into runtime metadata and verify dp works from outside the repo.
design: Keep the fix minimal: pyproject runtime dependency plus lock update if needed, then reinstall the editable uv tool and verify dp help and doctor from normal Codex PATH.
acceptance_criteria: 1. dp-codex package metadata includes all imports required by the dp console script. 2. uv tool install editable repo succeeds. 3. dp --help works from outside the repository. 4. dp doctor --json works in the repository through the globally installed command. 5. make check passes and changes are pushed.
close_reason: Moved jsonschema into runtime dependencies, refreshed uv.lock, reinstalled dp-codex as an editable uv tool, and verified dp resolves from /Users/rand/.local/bin, dp --help works outside the repo, dp doctor --json works in the repo, and UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache make check passes.

title: M7.4 Harden verification evidence beyond file existence
description: Strengthen dp verify and evidence conventions so proof artifacts contain commands, exit codes, hashes, and task or spec links instead of only checking path existence. Expected files: dp/core/verify.py, docs/schemas, tests, docs/specs/SPEC-70-04-evidence.md, docs/runbooks.
design: Use structured evidence records and backward-compatible manifest handling. Keep verification deterministic and local.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-70.04 defines evidence record semantics. 2. dp verify validates at least command, artifact, hash or link structure where configured. 3. Existing simple manifests remain supported or receive migration guidance. 4. Tests cover valid, incomplete, and failed evidence. 5. make check passes.

title: M7.3 Add Codex hooks and repo config integration
description: Add repo-scoped Codex integration for lightweight stop-time and pre-tool workflow checks. Expected files: .codex config or hooks files if appropriate, scripts or dp commands, tests, docs/specs/SPEC-70-03-codex-integration.md, docs/runbooks.
design: Keep hooks short, read-only by default, timeout-bounded, and aligned with Codex trust and approval behavior. Prefer dp doctor and targeted validation over full gates on every turn.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-70.03 defines Codex hook/config behavior. 2. Hook/config examples are repo-scoped and safe to trust deliberately. 3. Stop-time checks catch stale issue state or missing evidence without running heavy gates. 4. Docs explain how Codex users opt in and verify behavior. 5. make check passes.

title: M7.2 Add task claim ergonomics and context extraction
description: Add a first-class low-friction task intake path on top of current Beads claim semantics. Expected files: dp/cli/main.py, dp/providers/beads.py, tests/unit/test_cli_task.py, docs/runbooks/*.md, docs/specs/SPEC-70-02-task-intake.md.
design: Prefer a thin wrapper over bd ready --claim --json and bd update ID --claim. Parse and print issue context enough for Codex to know the scoped files without full-repo rediscovery.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-70.02 defines task claim/context behavior. 2. dp exposes claim/intake JSON that uses current Beads atomic claim semantics. 3. Missing context is surfaced as actionable guidance. 4. Tests cover ready-claim, known-id claim, and failure paths. 5. make check passes.

title: M7 Beads/Codex modernization and low-friction discipline
description: Modernize dp-codex around current Beads 1.0 behavior and current Codex integration surfaces while keeping the disciplined loop low-friction. Scope source: docs/EXECUTION-PLAN.md M7.
acceptance_criteria: M7 exit criteria in docs/EXECUTION-PLAN.md are satisfied: current Beads commands are capability-detected, Codex sessions use cheap health checks and scoped intake, enforcement separates read-only health from persistence, evidence is stronger than file existence, and pilots measure friction.
notes: 2026-06-27: Landed SPEC-70.07 Beads version-aware capability detection. dp doctor parses bd version, avoids probing removed bd sync for Beads 1.0+, reports sync_command_available=false without warning for current versions, and preserves legacy/unparseable sync probing for compatibility.

title: M7.1 Beads 1.0 compatibility and doctor baseline
description:
## Context
Files expected to change: docs/specs/SPEC-70-01-beads-doctor.md, dp/providers/beads.py, dp/cli/main.py, dp/enforcement/engine.py, dp/core/policy.py, dp/core/progress.py, tests/unit/test_beads_provider.py, tests/unit/test_cli_task.py, tests/unit/test_enforcement_engine.py, tests/unit/test_progress.py, tests/property/test_policy_properties.py, docs/EXECUTION-PLAN.md, README.md, AGENTS.md, docs/runbooks/*.md, docs/guides/*.md, docs/developer/contributor-handbook.md as needed. Research context: installed bd is 1.0.4; bd sync is no longer a command; bd ready --claim/update --claim/preflight/status/prime/bootstrap/vc/backup are current local surfaces; Codex docs recommend lean AGENTS, hooks/config/MCP/skills for integration.
design: First slice: add deterministic Beads health/capability detection and a read-only dp doctor surface; replace hard-coded bd sync assumptions with capability-aware guidance; keep writes explicit and avoid hidden state. Tests/specs precede implementation.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A SPEC-70.01 document defines Beads compatibility and doctor behavior before implementation. 2. Tests cover missing bd, missing .beads, empty/uninitialized Beads DB guidance, and stale bd sync handling. 3. dp exposes a read-only doctor/health path with stable JSON. 4. Enforcement and docs no longer require a nonexistent bd sync command. 5. make check passes.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-70.01 Beads doctor baseline with read-only Beads health detection, dp doctor JSON/text output, task_health enforcement replacing bd sync, updated docs and progress bootstrap guidance, and follow-up M7 backlog. Verified with dp doctor --json, trace validate/coverage, focused tests, lint, mypy, and UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache make check.

title: Set global Codex defaults for dp-codex and reposition README
description:
Objective: configure laptop-level Codex default behavior to exercise dp-codex workflows automatically where applicable, and update project README to present dp-codex as the product.

Files expected to change:
- ~/.codex/AGENTS.md
- README.md

Verification commands:
- make check
- scripts/run_pilot_migration.sh

Failure/rollback notes:
- If global instructions are too aggressive, scope them to repos that contain .beads and dp-policy.json markers.
acceptance_criteria: Codex default instructions are installed globally with dp-codex-first behavior (conditional by repo markers), README framing is product-oriented, and changes are committed/pushed with verification evidence.
close_reason: Installed laptop-wide Codex defaults in ~/.codex/AGENTS.md with conditional dp-codex activation by repo markers and orchestrator/worker discipline. Repositioned README to present dp-codex as the production project. Verified with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache make check and scripts/run_pilot_migration.sh.

title: Publish user guides by skill level and workflow
description:
Objective: produce practical guides for beginner, intermediate, and advanced users across end-to-end workflows.

Files expected to change:
- docs/guides/**
- docs/runbooks/**
- docs/pilot/**

Verification commands:
- make check
- scripts/run_pilot_migration.sh

Failure/rollback notes:
- If guide steps fail empirically, correct the guide before merge; do not preserve aspirational-only content.
acceptance_criteria: Users of varying skill levels can complete primary workflows using the guides without ad hoc maintainer support.
close_reason: Published role-based user guides for beginner/intermediate/advanced workflows (docs/guides/*), added CLI reference docs (docs/reference/*), and expanded troubleshooting runbook for operational recovery paths. Empirically validated workflows with scripts/run_pilot_migration.sh.

title: Author conceptual and internals documentation
description:
Objective: document mental models, system architecture, core module responsibilities, and data/control flow.

Files expected to change:
- docs/concepts/**
- docs/internals/**
- dp/** (only if references need correction)

Verification commands:
- make check
- uv run dp review --json

Failure/rollback notes:
- If internals docs drift from implementation, update docs immediately or revert conflicting sections.
acceptance_criteria: Concept and internals docs explain the why/how of all major workflows, with accurate command and module references.
close_reason: Authored conceptual docs (docs/concepts/*) and internals docs (docs/internals/*) covering mental model, governance, architecture, runtime semantics, data contracts, and testing strategy. Verified project integrity with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache make check.

title: Design documentation information architecture and index
description:
Objective: establish documentation structure, navigation, and canonical entry points.

Files expected to change:
- README.md
- docs/README.md
- docs/**

Verification commands:
- make check
- rg --files docs

Failure/rollback notes:
- Revert docs index files if navigation becomes inconsistent.
acceptance_criteria: A clear docs landing page exists, top-level README routes users by role, and all major doc domains are discoverable within 2 clicks.
close_reason: Implemented documentation information architecture with docs index (docs/README.md), runbook index (docs/runbooks/README.md), and updated root README routing by role and workflow. Verified coherence with UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache make check.

title: Documentation Excellence Pass
description: Objective: deliver comprehensive, high-quality project documentation spanning conceptual models, internals, developer workflows, and user guides for multiple skill levels. Non-goals: change runtime behavior unrelated to docs.
acceptance_criteria: Docs are complete, consistent in voice, cover all core workflows end-to-end, and include migration/troubleshooting paths for novice through expert users.
close_reason: Delivered comprehensive documentation suite across concepts, internals, developer standards, reference material, and role-based user guides with consistent voice, updated navigation, and empirically validated workflow steps.

title: Run v1 release readiness checklist
description: Validate final readiness gates and publish release decision.
acceptance_criteria: No open high-severity core defects; release checklist fully satisfied
close_reason: Executed v1 release readiness checklist and recorded decision in docs/release/v1-readiness.md. Readiness validated with passing quality gates, enforcement parity, pilot evidence, documented migration path, and no open high-severity core defects.

title: Execute pilot migration on target repository
description: Run planned migration from current workflow to dp-codex and collect evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot completes full loop without maintainer intervention
close_reason: Executed empirical pilot migration in isolated repository using scripts/run_pilot_migration.sh. Validated full disciplined loop (task lifecycle, trace, ADR, decompose, progress, enforcement, review, verify, close) without maintainer intervention; evidence captured in docs/evidence/2026-02-19/pilot-migration.txt.

title: Capture adoption friction and triage defects
description: Convert pilot friction into prioritized issues with ownership and severity.
acceptance_criteria: Critical/high defects identified with owners and remediation path
close_reason: Captured pilot friction and triaged defects with severity/owner in docs/pilot/friction-triage.md. Remediated identified high/medium blockers in trace parsing, review conflict detection, and enforcement environment handling; no open high-severity core defects remain.

title: Implement pre-push enforcement checks
description: Run sync/status safeguards before push, including policy-controlled responses.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-push checks deterministic and documented; failures actionable
close_reason: Implemented pre-push enforcement via dp enforce pre-push and hooks/pre-push with policy-controlled task_sync/review/verify checks, deterministic blocking semantics, and bypass path support. Verified empirically in pilot run where pre-push passes after clean commit (docs/evidence/2026-02-19/pilot-migration.txt).

title: Integrate enforcement checks into CI
description: Ensure enforcement logic is reproducible in CI without local hook assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: CI enforces same policy-relevant checks as local workflows
close_reason: Integrated enforcement parity into CI by running dp enforce pre-commit and dp enforce pre-push with the versioned dp-policy.json after make check. Added default verify manifest and updated command runbooks to keep local hook and CI behavior aligned.

title: Implement policy config schema (strict/guided/minimal)
description: Define repository-configurable enforcement policy with per-check overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Policy schema validates and supports strict/guided/minimal with per-check overrides
close_reason: Implemented policy config schema and validator supporting strict/guided/minimal modes plus per-check boolean overrides (core module dp/core/policy.py, schema docs/schemas/policy.schema.json, CLI command dp policy validate). Added unit/CLI tests and policy runbook docs; verified with make check and end-to-end dp policy validate --json run.

title: Implement pre-commit enforcement checks
description: Run configured quality and trace checks before commit with policy-controlled blocking.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-commit behavior matches selected policy level
close_reason: Implemented policy-driven pre-commit enforcement via dp enforce pre-commit with lint/typecheck/test/trace gates, deterministic JSON/text reports, and trace coverage post-evaluation. Added hook integration, unit/CLI tests, and empirical evidence (docs/evidence/2026-02-19/enforce-pre-commit.json, quality-gates.txt).

title: Implement progress snapshot and watch-mode triggers
description: Generate markdown+json progress reports and watch-mode trigger evaluation.
acceptance_criteria: dp progress supports one-shot and watch mode with trigger reasoning
close_reason: Implemented dp progress with one-shot markdown+json snapshot generation and watch-mode trigger evaluation with explicit reasoning (working-tree-dirty, ready-issue-growth, spec-count-change). Added progress core module, CLI command, tests, and runbook docs. Verified with make check and uv run dp progress --json.

title: Port decompose engine with configurable context window
description: Implement or port decomposition graph generation and ensure context-window parameterization.
acceptance_criteria: dp decompose emits DAG-validated plan output with context-window controls
close_reason: Implemented context-window-configurable decomposition engine in dp/core/decompose.py and dp decompose CLI command with deterministic DAG-validated plan output (text + --json), including item splitting when estimates exceed window. Added unit/CLI tests and decompose runbook docs; verified with make check and uv run dp decompose ... --json.

title: Add integration scenario for full feature workflow
description: Cover task->spec->trace->review->verify sequence in integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: At least one end-to-end scenario passes in CI
close_reason: Added integration scenario tests covering task->spec->trace->review->verify flow in tests/integration/test_full_feature_workflow.py, including task discovery, trace coverage/validation, deterministic review summary, and verify success outcome. Scenario runs in CI through make check and passes.

title: Implement deterministic checklist review engine
description: Build structured review checks with blocking/advisory categorization and file/line findings.
acceptance_criteria: dp review emits deterministic findings and commit-readiness summary
close_reason: Implemented deterministic checklist review engine in dp/core/review.py with blocking/advisory findings (worktree-dirty, merge-conflict markers, TODO/FIXME), file/line metadata, sorted output, and commit-readiness summary; added dp review CLI (text + --json) with stable exit behavior and runbook docs. Verified by make check and uv run dp review --json.

title: Implement goal-backward verification engine
description: Implement truths/artifacts/links verification and overall verified/incomplete/failed outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: dp verify reports per-level results and stable exit codes
close_reason: Implemented goal-backward verification engine in dp/core/verify.py with truths/artifacts/links level evaluation, per-level statuses, rollup outcomes (verified/incomplete/failed), and stable exit codes via new dp verify command. Added JSON/text output, unit + CLI tests, and verify runbook documentation. Verified with make check.

title: Implement ADR lifecycle commands
description: Support adr create/list/show/update workflows with status transitions and file conventions.
acceptance_criteria: dp adr supports proposal->accepted->superseded/deprecated lifecycle
close_reason: Implemented ADR lifecycle command set (dp adr create/list/show/update) with canonical docs/adr/ADR-XXXX-<slug>.md conventions, front-matter metadata, and enforced lifecycle transitions proposal->accepted->superseded/deprecated. Added core and CLI tests plus ADR workflow runbook documentation; verified via make check.

title: Handle missing bd and uninitialized .beads failures
description: Provide deterministic failure behavior and remediation hints when tracker dependencies are unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: All tracker-unavailable scenarios return actionable errors and non-zero exit codes
close_reason: Added deterministic tracker-unavailable handling: dp task now returns actionable errors for missing bd (exit 127) and uninitialized .beads context (exit 2) via dp/providers/beads.py and CLI handling in dp/cli/main.py. Added provider and CLI tests for these failure scenarios and verified with make check.

title: Implement dp task ready/show/update/close wrappers
description: Create Beads-backed command handlers for core task lifecycle operations.
acceptance_criteria: All core task subcommands operate against bd with predictable output and errors
close_reason: Implemented Beads-backed dp task wrappers for ready/show/update/close in dp/cli/main.py using provider abstraction dp/providers/beads.py, with predictable error handling and exit codes. Added unit tests for command delegation and failure modes in tests/unit/test_cli_task.py, and verified with make check plus uv run dp task ready.

title: Implement dp task discover and dependency linking
description: Support discovered work creation linked via discovered-from/blocking relationships.
acceptance_criteria: Discovered tasks are linked and visible in dependency graph
close_reason: Implemented dp task discover wrapper with automatic discovered-from and blocks linkage to source issue, plus optional metadata passthrough (description, acceptance, priority, labels, assignee, dry-run). Added CLI delegation tests for base and optional discover flows and verified with make check plus uv run dp task discover ... --dry-run.

title: Add parser and validator edge-case test suite
description: Cover malformed IDs, duplicate IDs, mixed-language traces, and large-file scanning boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover parser/validator edge cases and pass in CI
close_reason: Added edge-case unit suite in tests/unit/test_traceability_edge_cases.py covering malformed IDs, duplicate spec ID dedupe behavior, mixed-language trace parsing, and large-file boundary scanning; suite passes in CI-equivalent local gates via make check.

title: Implement trace marker parser for @trace SPEC-XX.YY
description: Parse trace markers in code and tests, capturing file and line metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Trace parser resolves markers and locations for supported languages
close_reason: Implemented trace marker parser in dp/core/trace_parser.py for @trace SPEC-XX.YY with file/line/column metadata and supported-language suffix filtering; exported through dp.core and added unit tests in tests/unit/test_trace_parser.py for ordering, language support, invalid markers, and multi-marker lines. Verified with make check.

title: Build coverage engine and coverage report command
description: Compute covered/uncovered specs by matching parsed spec IDs against trace markers.
acceptance_criteria: dp trace coverage returns totals, covered count, uncovered list, and JSON output
close_reason: Implemented trace coverage engine in dp/core/coverage.py and wired new CLI command dp trace coverage in dp/cli/main.py (human + --json output with total_specs, covered_count, and uncovered_specs); added unit tests for coverage math and CLI output, and verified with make check plus uv run dp trace coverage --json.

title: Build strict trace validation command
description: Fail on unresolved spec references, malformed markers, and missing target specs.
acceptance_criteria: dp trace validate exits non-zero on unresolved/malformed refs with actionable diagnostics
close_reason: Implemented strict trace validation pipeline: trace parser now emits malformed marker diagnostics, new core validator detects unresolved references vs spec IDs, and new CLI command dp trace validate returns actionable diagnostics and non-zero exit on failures (plus --json output). Added unit coverage for parser/validator/CLI behaviors and verified with make check.

title: Implement spec ID parser for [SPEC-XX.YY]
description: Parse normative spec IDs across docs/spec files with deterministic ordering and dedupe.
acceptance_criteria: Parser returns stable list of spec IDs and source locations
close_reason: Implemented typed SPEC ID parser in dp/core/spec_parser.py with deterministic ordering, deduped ID output, and source location capture (path/line/column); added unit coverage in tests/unit/test_spec_parser.py and verified with make check.

title: Define canonical developer commands
description: Standardize test/lint/typecheck/format command entrypoints for local and CI consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Single documented command path exists for each quality gate
close_reason: Added canonical quality-gate entrypoints (test/lint/typecheck/format/check) in Makefile, established deterministic toolchain/config in pyproject.toml, documented the single command path in README and docs/runbooks/developer-commands.md, and validated with make lint, make typecheck, make test, and make check.

title: Implement CI quality-gate pipeline
description: Add CI workflow that runs lint, typecheck, and tests with fail-fast behavior.
acceptance_criteria: CI executes same command set as local development and fails on gate violations
close_reason: Added GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml that checks out code, sets up Python+uv, runs uv sync --dev, and executes make check so CI uses the same local quality-gate command path with fail-fast job behavior. Verified locally with make check.

title: Bootstrap repository skeleton and package layout
description: Create canonical directories and initial package/module boundaries aligned to EXECUTION-PLAN target architecture.
acceptance_criteria: Repository tree includes dp/core, dp/cli, dp/providers, dp/enforcement, hooks, scripts, tests, docs/runbooks
close_reason: Created baseline repository skeleton with dp/core, dp/cli, dp/providers, dp/enforcement, hooks, scripts, tests (unit/integration/e2e), and docs/runbooks/specs/adr; added package boundary __init__.py files and verified with python3 -m compileall dp. Canonical make targets are tracked in existing issue dpcx-egm.1.2.

title: M6 Pilot migration and hardening
description: Run pilot adoption, capture friction, close critical defects, and finalize v1 readiness.
acceptance_criteria: Pilot executes full loop without maintainer intervention; no open high-severity core defects
close_reason: Completed M6 pilot migration and hardening: pilot executed end-to-end with evidence, friction triaged and remediated, migration/troubleshooting docs published, and v1 readiness checklist passed.

title: M3 ADR, review, and goal-backward verify
description: Add ADR lifecycle commands plus deterministic review and verification engines.
acceptance_criteria: dp adr/review/verify produce consistent outputs and integration tests cover full feature workflow
close_reason: M3 completed: dp adr lifecycle commands, deterministic dp review, and goal-backward dp verify are implemented with consistent outputs, JSON schemas, and integration coverage for the full task->spec->trace->review->verify workflow. Verified through make check.

title: M4 Decompose and progress workflows (Codex-optimized)
description: Implement decomposition and reporting workflows tuned for Codex context windows and session recovery.
acceptance_criteria: dp decompose emits validated execution graph; dp progress emits markdown+json; fresh session orientation <=2 min
close_reason: M4 completed: dp decompose now emits context-window-calibrated DAG plans with Codex presets and heuristic splitting/merging, and dp progress emits markdown+json snapshots with watch trigger reasoning plus agent-bootstrap orientation content. Verified through make check and end-to-end command runs.

title: M5 Enforcement and governance layer
description: Implement strict/guided/minimal policy using git hooks + CI, with auditable bypass path.
acceptance_criteria: Enforcement reproducible in local and CI; policy schema supports per-check overrides and documented bypass
close_reason: Completed M5 enforcement/governance layer: pre-commit and pre-push enforcement implemented, policy schema + overrides active, hook scripts added, CI parity enforced, bypass protocol documented and audited. Verified with make check and pilot evidence runs.

title: M2 Task workflow layer over Beads
description: Implement unified dp task command surface mapped to Beads lifecycle and dependency operations.
acceptance_criteria: Task lifecycle ready->in_progress->close works end-to-end via dp task commands with actionable errors
close_reason: M2 task workflow layer completed: dp task lifecycle wrappers (ready/show/update/close/discover), status/priority normalization, structured --json mode, and tracker-availability error handling are implemented and verified.

title: SPEC-80.21 primary spec intake UX and source adapters
description: Improve campaign init intake for real human workflows: support explicit dry-run/preview, better diagnostics, and source adapters such as URL import where appropriate, while preserving deterministic gates and local artifact provenance.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.21 documents primary-spec source semantics, dry-run/write behavior, provenance, and safety constraints. 2. dp campaign init can preview planned artifacts without requiring --write and can clearly explain why a primary spec needs refinement. 3. Path-or-URL behavior is either implemented with provenance and deterministic write gates or deliberately narrowed in SPEC-80 with accurate docs. 4. Generated artifact names and collision handling are ergonomic for repeated human use. 5. Tests cover dry-run, local path, unsupported/allowed URL behavior, collisions, docs, schemas, and make check.

title: SPEC-80.20 managed supervised loop and agent launch adapters
description: After the manual and one-step supervised protocols are proven, add thin managed-loop and optional launch adapters over the same dp campaign/loop/goal/evidence commands without creating a general autonomous agent framework.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.20 defines the difference between one-step supervised run, managed supervised loop, and direct agent launch. 2. Any loop mode stops on blockers, no-ready work, unsafe scope, failed evidence, stale leases, or budget exhaustion. 3. dp agent launch --driver codex, if implemented, is a thin adapter that emits/starts the same contract and never becomes the verification judge. 4. The command is opt-in, explicit, and not used by hooks or CI. 5. Tests cover stop conditions and no model/network calls in gates; docs and make check pass.

title: SPEC-80.13 supervised campaign run step
description: Implement the first supervised runner slice as a thin adapter over the manual dp protocol: dp campaign run validates a CampaignManifest, selects the current loop, claims one next ready goal, emits a Codex package, and stops. It must not launch an autonomous background runner or make verification judgments.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.13 documents the supervised single-step runner protocol and invariants before implementation. 2. dp campaign run <campaign.json> --driver codex --supervised --json validates the campaign, resolves the current loop, claims the next ready goal with a lease, emits the Codex package, and returns explicit lifecycle commands and stop conditions. 3. The command is a thin adapter over existing campaign status, loop next, and goal state behavior; it does not execute evidence, verify goals, launch Codex, or loop indefinitely. 4. No-ready-work and unsupported-driver cases return stable structured JSON and explicit exit codes. 5. Tests, schema/docs/README updates, Beads updates, make check, dp trace validate/coverage, dp verify, and dp doctor pass.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.13 supervised campaign run step: dp campaign run --driver codex --supervised validates campaign state, resolves the current loop, claims one ready goal through loop.next, emits a Codex handoff package, and stops without launching agents or executing evidence. Verified with pytest tests/test_campaign_run.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, trace validate/coverage, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp doctor --json, and bd --readonly status --json.

title: SPEC-80.12 agent-mediated LLM refinement protocol
description: Implement the next SPEC-80 LLM refinement slice without putting model calls in gates: dp campaign refine emits an agent-facing LLM request package for the calling agent/provider, then imports an explicit LLM response artifact through deterministic validation and provenance.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.12 documents the agent-mediated LLM refinement protocol, response schema, provenance fields, and invariants before implementation. 2. dp campaign refine <campaign.json> --llm --json emits a stable request package for the calling agent provider without writing campaign artifacts or running gates as model judgment. 3. dp campaign refine <campaign.json> --llm-response <response.json> --write --json imports only a valid response artifact, records model provenance, updates campaign/goal/evidence refinement metadata, and preserves draft state. 4. Invalid response artifacts fail deterministically with structured errors and no partial writes. 5. Tests cover request emission, valid response import, invalid goal ids, raw shell denial in model-proposed evidence, provenance shape, docs, and make check.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.12 agent-mediated LLM refinement protocol. Added dp campaign refine --llm request emission for the calling agent provider, --llm-response import with prompt-hash binding and deterministic validation, LLM provenance recording, draft campaign/goal/evidence refinement metadata import, raw-shell evidence denial, response/output schemas, spec/docs/README updates, and tests for request emission, valid import, unknown goal rejection, raw shell rejection, and schema validation. Verified with targeted pytest, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, dp verify --json, and dp doctor --json.

title: SPEC-80.11 deterministic campaign refine and Beads materialization
description: Implement the next SPEC-80 campaign control-plane slice: dp campaign refine consumes a draft CampaignManifest plus compiler/needs_refinement artifacts and writes deterministic child specs, ADR stubs, refined GoalContracts/EvidencePlans, and optional Beads epic/issues without LLM calls. It must also define the provenance contract for future LLM-assisted refinement using the calling agent/provider boundary.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.11 documents deterministic refine semantics, Beads materialization rules, and future LLM provenance fields before coding. 2. dp campaign refine <campaign.json> supports dry-run and --write JSON outputs with stable exit codes. 3. Deterministic refine writes child spec stubs, ADR stubs for decision nodes, evidence-plan updates/stubs, and refined goal metadata from compiler signals without changing campaign readiness to verified. 4. With an explicit Beads write flag, dp creates or records Beads epic/issues idempotently enough for repeated runs to avoid duplicate repo artifact references. 5. Future --llm is explicitly authoring-only and provenance-gated; if not implemented in this slice, it fails with a structured not-implemented response rather than silently ignoring the flag. 6. Tests cover dry-run, write, Beads dry/proposed behavior or mocked creation, provenance shape, docs, and make check.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.11 deterministic campaign refine and Beads materialization. Added docs/specs/SPEC-80-11-campaign-refine-and-beads-materialization.md, dp campaign refine dry-run/write/create-beads/llm-fail-closed command surface, deterministic child spec/ADR materialization, GoalContract and EvidencePlan refinement metadata, calling-agent LLM provenance contract, campaign-refine JSON schema, docs/README updates, and tests. Verified with targeted pytest, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, dp verify --json, and dp doctor --json.

title: SPEC-80.09 deterministic primary-spec semantic signals
description: Implement the first bounded SPEC-80.09 compiler slice: deterministic Markdown signal extraction for primary specs so campaign init can expose requirement, evidence, decision, blocker, and dependency cues without LLM calls or semantic overclaiming.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-80.09 documents the deterministic compiler contract and formal invariants before implementation. 2. dp campaign init output includes a stable compiler analysis object with mode, no-LLM flags, node classifications, refinement states, extracted signal cues, and summary counts. 3. Generated campaign, loop, goal, and refinement-marker artifacts carry enough compiler provenance for a future Codex session to understand why nodes are draft or implementation candidates. 4. The command remains draft-only, deterministic, local, and free of LLM calls, Beads writes, evidence execution, or inferred dependency edges. 5. Fixtures and tests cover requirements/evidence/decision/dependency cues, sparse specs, schema validation, docs, and make check.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.09 deterministic primary-spec semantic signals. Added SPEC-80.09 contract and formal invariants; dp campaign init now emits a stable compiler object with deterministic Markdown signal extraction, no-LLM flags, node classifications, refinement states, signal cues, and summary counts. Generated campaign, loop, goal, and needs_refinement artifacts carry compiler provenance while remaining draft and without inferred dependency edges, Beads writes, evidence execution, or LLM calls. Verified with pytest tests/test_campaign_init.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, and dp verify --json.

title: SPEC-80.10 Evidence verification to goal completion
description: Integrate controlled evidence run artifacts with goal lifecycle completion so dp can move from evidence_pending to verified only when deterministic evidence validation succeeds.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Spec or ADR defines evidence run artifact provenance, freshness, and goal/evidence-plan matching rules. 2. dp goal complete or a dedicated verification command consumes evidence run output and records verified only when the run is valid, successful, and matches the goal contract. 3. Loop dependencies unlock from verified events, not agent narration or evidence_pending. 4. Tests cover passing evidence, failed evidence, mismatched goal/evidence ids, stale or missing run artifacts, and self-report non-proof. 5. make check, dp trace validate/coverage, and dp verify pass.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.10 evidence verification into goal state. Added docs/specs/SPEC-80-10-evidence-verification.md, dp goal verify, evidence-run provenance hashing, stable evidence-run schema updates, loop/goal emitted verify commands, docs and README updates, and tests covering passing evidence, failed evidence, mismatched goal id, stale EvidencePlan hash, missing run artifact, and self-report non-proof. Verified with targeted pytest, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, dp verify --json, dp doctor --json, and bd --readonly status --json.

title: SPEC-80.06 Campaign manifest lint status and recover
description: Implement campaign manifest lint/status/recover surfaces after goal contracts, state, emission, loops, and evidence lint are established.
acceptance_criteria: 1. CampaignManifest schema is documented. 2. dp campaign lint/status/recover emit stable JSON. 3. Recover reconstructs visible state from repo artifacts and event logs without chat memory. 4. Tests cover valid, blocked, incomplete, and missing-artifact campaigns. 5. make check passes.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.06 CampaignManifest lint/status/recover with schema docs, golden fixtures, CLI tests, schema validation, deterministic artifact closure, read-only status/recover, and verified via targeted tests, make check, trace validation/coverage, and dp verify.

title: SPEC-80.07 Primary spec campaign scaffold
description: Implement conservative dp campaign init --primary-spec <path> --write --json scaffold mode without LLM refinement or autonomous running.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The command hashes the primary spec and creates campaign, loop, draft goals, and needs_refinement markers conservatively. 2. Ambiguity routes to needs_specification, needs_decision, or needs_validator. 3. Generated artifacts must pass deterministic lint or remain draft with reasons. 4. Tests cover deterministic extraction and incomplete scaffold output. 5. make check passes.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.07 conservative primary-spec campaign scaffold. dp campaign init --primary-spec <path> --write --json now hashes a local primary spec, extracts Markdown headings, writes draft CampaignManifest/LoopLedger/GoalContract/EvidencePlan artifacts plus needs_refinement markers, refuses changed-artifact overwrites, and lints generated artifacts without LLM calls, Beads writes, evidence execution, or semantic planning. Verified with pytest tests/test_campaign_init.py tests/test_campaign_manifest.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, temp-dir scaffold smoke, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate/coverage, and dp verify.

title: SPEC-80.04 Loop ledger next-goal protocol
description: Implement loop ledger lint/status/next only after goal contracts, goal state, and prompt emission are solid.
acceptance_criteria: 1. docs/specs/SPEC-80-04-loop-ledgers.md defines ledger schema and scheduler rules. 2. dp loop lint/status/next support acyclic dependencies, ready unclaimed nodes, claim leases, blocked-node skipping, and Codex package emission. 3. pytest tests/test_loop_ledger.py and make check pass.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic LoopLedger lint/status/next with documented SPEC-80.04 schema, stable loop-lint JSON schema, acyclic dependency validation, valid GoalContract references, blocked and claimed node skipping, verified-event dependency unlocks, claim leases through existing goal events, and Codex package emission. Verified with dp loop lint tests/fixtures/loops/valid_spec_80_04.json --json, dp loop status tests/fixtures/loops/valid_spec_80_04.json --json, pytest tests/test_loop_ledger.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate --json, dp trace coverage --json, dp verify --json, and dp doctor --json.

title: M7.6 Evaluate skill MCP and plugin packaging
description: Evaluate whether dp-codex should expose repo context through a Codex skill, MCP server, plugin package, or remain CLI-only. Expected files: docs/specs/SPEC-70-06-codex-packaging.md, docs/adr, optional .agents skill or plugin scaffolding only if justified.
design: Start with an ADR-quality comparison. Prefer local skill instructions for repeated workflows; use MCP for structured tool state only when it clearly improves safety or ergonomics; use plugin packaging only when stable distribution is needed.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-70.06 or ADR compares CLI, skill, MCP, and plugin options against safety, friction, and maintenance cost. 2. Recommendation identifies the minimal useful integration surface. 3. Any scaffold added is tested or documented with install and trust steps. 4. No network-dependent runtime is introduced without explicit rationale. 5. make check passes if files change.

title: M7.5 Add flow pilots and friction metrics
description: Add flow-level pilots or eval fixtures that measure whether the disciplined process improves setup recovery, task claim latency, evidence completeness, and false-positive enforcement friction. Expected files: scripts or tests, docs/specs/SPEC-70-05-flow-evals.md, docs/pilot or docs/runbooks.
design: Prefer deterministic local fixtures over live services. Capture both old-flow failure modes and new-flow expected behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1. SPEC-70.05 defines flow metrics and pilot scenarios. 2. At least one automated or scripted pilot exercises doctor, claim, implement, verify, close, and preflight. 3. Pilot output reports friction metrics in stable JSON or markdown. 4. Docs explain how to run and interpret the pilot. 5. make check passes.

title: Documentation QA, consistency, and voice pass
description:
Objective: enforce consistent voice, precision, and cross-link integrity across all docs; include tasteful style and dry humor without reducing clarity.

Files expected to change:
- README.md
- docs/**

Verification commands:
- make check
- rg -n "TODO|FIXME|TBD" docs README.md

Failure/rollback notes:
- If tone becomes unclear or noisy, prioritize precision and reduce stylistic content.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation has consistent voice, precise language, and coherent cross-references; no placeholder text remains.
close_reason: Completed editorial QA pass for voice and consistency, added documentation style guidance (docs/developer/documentation-style.md), and removed placeholder markers. Verified with rg -n "TODO|FIXME|TBD|stub|mock" and UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache make check.

title: Publish migration and troubleshooting guides
description: Document migration checklist, rollback considerations, and troubleshooting for adopters.
acceptance_criteria: External team can execute migration guide without ad hoc support
close_reason: Published migration and troubleshooting guidance for external adopters in docs/runbooks/migration-guide.md and docs/runbooks/troubleshooting.md, with bootstrap, smoke validation, rollback, and failure-recovery paths documented.

title: Document bypass protocol and audit conventions
description: Define emergency bypass process and required audit trail artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Bypass path documented with explicit logging requirements
close_reason: Documented emergency bypass protocol and audit conventions in enforcement/policy runbooks, including required DP_BYPASS_REASON usage and JSONL audit fields in .dp/bypass-log.jsonl. Added bypass logging behavior in enforcement engine and verified with hook invocation.

title: Calibrate Codex sizing presets and splitting heuristics
description: Tune token-budget heuristics for Codex-oriented context windows and split/merge rules.
acceptance_criteria: Codex preset documented and validated against representative decomposition cases
close_reason: Calibrated decompose sizing with Codex context presets (codex-small/medium/large), added preset resolution API and split/merge heuristics, exposed --preset in dp decompose, and documented behavior in decompose runbook. Added representative-case tests validating preset impact and heuristics; verified with make check and uv run dp decompose --preset codex-medium --json.

title: Add agent bootstrap section to progress reports
description: Generate concise context-loading instructions optimized for fresh Codex sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh session can orient from latest progress report with minimal repository scanning
close_reason: Added agent bootstrap section to progress outputs (JSON + Markdown) with concise summary, triggered checks, and prioritized next commands for fresh Codex sessions. Updated progress report generation logic, tests, and runbook guidance; verified with make check and uv run dp progress --json.

title: Define JSON schemas for review and verify outputs
description: Formalize output contracts for automation and CI consumption.
acceptance_criteria: Schema-validated JSON output available for review/verify commands
close_reason: Defined formal JSON Schemas for review and verify outputs (docs/schemas/review-output.schema.json and docs/schemas/verify-output.schema.json), added schema contract runbook, and added tests that validate real dp review --json / dp verify --json payloads against those schemas using jsonschema. Verified with make check.

title: Add structured JSON output mode for dp task
description: Provide machine-readable output schema for all task operations.
acceptance_criteria: dp task subcommands support --json with stable schema
close_reason: Added --json mode for all dp task subcommands with a stable output envelope (command, ok, exit_code, data, stderr, error, optional raw_output), forwarding bd --json data and preserving deterministic error semantics. Added CLI schema/error tests and documented the schema in docs/runbooks/task-json-output.md. Verified with make check and uv run dp task ready --json.

title: Normalize status and priority semantics
description: Define canonical mapping and validation for status/priority values between dp and bd.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid statuses/priorities fail with clear guidance; mappings documented
close_reason: Added canonical status/priority normalization (with aliases and strict validation) in dp/core/task_normalization.py, integrated into dp task update/discover so invalid values fail fast with actionable guidance, and documented mappings in docs/runbooks/task-normalization.md. Added normalization and CLI error-path tests; verified with make check and end-to-end invalid-status run.

title: Author environment bootstrap runbook
description: Document first-time setup and troubleshooting for contributors and Codex sessions.
acceptance_criteria: New contributor can bootstrap from runbook without tribal knowledge
close_reason: Added docs/runbooks/environment-bootstrap.md with first-time setup prerequisites, deterministic bootstrap steps, Codex session startup commands, and troubleshooting guidance; linked it from README and re-verified quality gates with make check.

title: SPEC-80.09 Semantic compiler, LLM refinement, and supervised runner
description: Evaluate and implement the remaining SPEC-80 campaign intelligence only after the deterministic manual protocol is stable: semantic primary-spec-to-campaign compilation, authoring-time LLM refinement with provenance, and a supervised runner as a thin adapter over the same dp goal/loop/evidence protocol.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Semantic primary-spec compilation is specified before coding and does not rely on token-count slicing as semantic planning. 2. Compiler output remains draft until deterministic campaign, loop, goal, and evidence gates pass. 3. Any LLM-assisted command is authoring-only, records provenance, and is gated by deterministic lint before ready state. 4. The supervised runner is a thin adapter over manual dp protocol and never makes verification judgments. 5. Hooks, CI, and blocking gates remain LLM-free. 6. Tests, docs, schemas, Beads updates, and make check pass for any implementation.
notes:
2026-06-27: Split out dpcx-pb5.9.1 for the deterministic primary-spec semantic-signal compiler slice. Parent remains open for full semantic campaign compilation, authoring-time LLM refinement with provenance, and supervised runner work.
2026-06-27: dpcx-pb5.9.1 landed deterministic semantic-signal extraction for dp campaign init. Remaining scope on this issue is authoring-time semantic refinement into child artifacts, optional LLM refinement with provenance and deterministic lint back-pressure, and any supervised runner as a thin adapter over manual dp protocol.
2026-06-27: dpcx-pb5.9.2 landed SPEC-80.11 deterministic campaign refine. dp campaign refine now dry-runs or writes child spec/ADR stubs, GoalContract/EvidencePlan refinement metadata, and optional Beads epic/issues with --create-beads while preserving draft state. --llm fails closed as explicit future authoring mode with calling-agent provenance fields. Remaining scope: implement actual LLM-assisted refinement with deterministic lint back-pressure, richer semantic graph refinement, and any supervised runner as a thin adapter over the manual dp protocol.
2026-06-27: dpcx-pb5.9.3 landed SPEC-80.12 agent-mediated LLM refinement. dp campaign refine --llm now emits a prompt-bound request package for the calling agent provider/model; dp campaign refine --llm-response <response.json> --write imports only a valid response artifact, records provider/model provenance, updates campaign/goal/evidence draft refinement metadata, rejects unknown goals and raw-shell evidence proposals, and preserves draft state. Remaining scope: richer semantic graph hardening and any supervised runner as a thin adapter over manual dp protocol.
2026-06-27: dpcx-pb5.9.4 landed SPEC-80.13 supervised campaign run step. dp campaign run --driver codex --supervised now validates campaign state, resolves the current loop, claims one ready goal through loop.next, emits the Codex handoff package and lifecycle commands, and stops without launching agents, executing evidence, or verifying completion. Remaining scope on this parent is richer semantic graph hardening and any later direct launch/background runner work only after the supervised protocol remains reliable.

title: SPEC-80.08 Evidence executor safety model
description: Design and implement dp evidence run only after evidence lint exists, with argv-only execution, allowlists, timeouts, controlled cwd/env, and typed assertions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ADR or spec defines executor security model. 2. dp evidence run never uses shell execution. 3. Execution uses allowlisted argv arrays, timeouts, controlled cwd/env, and typed assertions. 4. Tests cover denial cases and successful local fixtures. 5. make check passes.
close_reason: Implemented SPEC-80.08 controlled evidence executor. Added SPEC, ADR update, stable evidence-run JSON schema, dp evidence run CLI, argv-only shell=False execution behind deterministic lint, controlled cwd/env, declared timeouts, typed assertions, denial fixtures, success/failure fixtures, schema tests, README/reference/runbook updates. Verified with dp evidence run tests/fixtures/evidence/valid_run_goal_lint.json --json, pytest tests/test_evidence_run.py tests/test_evidence_lint.py tests/unit/test_output_schemas.py, make test, make lint, make typecheck, make check, dp trace validate/coverage, dp verify, dp doctor, and bd --readonly status.
